title: The Reiquarium and Destrudo
wikitext:
[]: The Reiquarium and Destrudo             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 05, 2013 9:16 pm I was doing some NGE searching, one of my favorite pastimes, when I came across the Reiquarium scene from episode 23 (I think, anyway.) Y'know, gun to Ritsuko's back, walls light up, lots of naked Reis, they all crumble and die. I took it at face value the first 8 times, but I took a few freezeframes this time for curiosity's sake. So, there were originally 14 Rei clones. At the time of their destruction, there were 11 left in the tank. I found this shocking; it looked like there were so many more in the series. However, Ritsuko's pager specifically designates that Reis 004-014 were in the tank. I was secretly hoping that there would be twelve, mainly so I could bitch and moan about how often that number appears. However, I am curious as to why there weren't more clones. Only having 14 seems like a risky business. On to the next order of business. I was never sure of how they all 'sploded at the same time. For a while, I assumed internal bombs or some other kind of deterioration device. Ritsuko's pager provided the answer again. I was pretty shocked upon the answer. The clones were destroyed via a Destrudo Release, also known as manually breaking the A.T. Field. I'm more interested in the fact that these clones had a Destrudo in the first place. In order to have a Destrudo, it is required that you transfer your libido to an outside object and then fail to receive love from anything else. Wait, the Rei clones have a libido? Not really important, but I don't see the appeal to fucking something without a soul. Back to the point. With the release of the Destrudo, the ego borders break down, the Death Impulse is transmitted, and you undergo a simultaneous mental and physical collapse. Apparently, Nerv had the power to do this all along. Perhaps the Rei clones had mental implants to control emotion, thus permitting the Destrudo release. I'm assuming that everything develops a libido, whether it has a soul or not. I'm just going to say that the Reiquarium is pretty awkward as it is, without bringing anything else into it. Sadly, Nerv did, and now we need to analyze it until our brains try to escape out through our ears.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Feb 05, 2013 9:22 pm They had rudeamentary intelligence able to recognize Shinji, it wouldn't be a shocker if they shared Rei's desire to return to nothing since this is painted as a fundamental human trait in NGE. Of course for all we know it was created artificially or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 06, 2013 12:50 am Somehow I always got the impression that those would just desintegrate unless something kept them from doing so, and Ritsuko just turned that something off, as the uses of the words "locked" and "release" implies. They had rudeamentary intelligence able to recognize Shinji

[]:             Quote Post by fizz450_03 » Wed Feb 20, 2013 1:04 am i'm thinking the pressure in the tank went up exponentially and well crushed them? but they weren't really crushed...more like crumbled

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 20, 2013 1:41 am I agree that it seems as though Ritsuko released some mechanism that had been keeping the bodies together in the first place. Without souls or an AT Field to maintain their shape, their bodies need to somehow be maintained artificially. The fact that they're floating in LCL is probably also relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Feb 20, 2013 2:16 am The only thing she could have released is the negative energy born of Rei's desire to return to nothing. The remote makes this really, really straight forward. Where exactly it comes from is the only real question here.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 20, 2013 2:57 am Sachi wrote: I agree that it seems as though Ritsuko released some mechanism that had been keeping the bodies together in the first place. Without souls or an AT Field to maintain their shape, their bodies need to somehow be maintained artificially. The fact that they're floating in LCL is probably also relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Feb 20, 2013 3:03 pm Monk Ed wrote: That's not what seems to be implied by a "destrudo release". Destrudo seems to be something that actively breaks something else down . In all other places that word appears it seems to describe something bad, especially in relation to things like "egos"/AT Fields.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 20, 2013 8:08 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Destrudo is the human death impulse that is countered by libido. The only time that a destrudo can actually manifest is when the person in question projects its libido onto something that is unable to return it. Like I said, I'm more interested in where the Reis got the libido in the first place, since a soul is necessary. If there's only one Lilith soul to go around, what the hell is going on?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Feb 20, 2013 8:33 pm RCB specifically says it is the impulse of self destruction. It just seems to have an actual measurable effect when Evas are involved. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Feb 27, 2013 3:46 pm I always assumed that Rei needs constant attention to stop her from falling apart anyway, and that the destruction of the Reiquarium was just releasing some kind of virus into the tank to biodegrade them. My other theory is this. The reason Rei and her clones are so fragile is that her existence is so unnatural that even without a soul, her body is unable to maintain its structure because it is constantly screaming for death at the molecular level , hence the Destrudo theory (in effect, her genetic makeup is so artificial and hellishly wrong that it actually generates an anti-AT field). An artificial AT field is holding them together in the tank to prevent the tortured bodies from disintegrating out of sheer agony at the subatomic level. This could be why they seemed to giggle and smile when they are finally allowed to die and return to nothingness. Whilst the Rei body that contains her/Lilith's soul (Rei 1, 2 or 3) is held together by her soul's AT field (as all the incarnations of Rei are technically speaking the same person), the anti-AT field generated by her body's sheer desire to just stop existing negatively affects her personality, as she can feel a fraction of how fundamentally wrong her existence is, leaving her with no self worth and a desire to return to oblivion. This could also be why Rei's arm fell of in End of Evangelion; whilst standing so close to Lilith, her soul was begging to get out of the horrible shell it had been placed in and return to its own body, causing Rei's body to break down. Damnit, that was more morbid than I intended.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Feb 27, 2013 4:11 pm Interesting, but what about Kaworu? He's trapped in a Lilim body, which should be significantly worse than 'tis for Rei, who is the mother of Lilim. And yet Kaworu seems healthy, or at least he doesn't show any angst about it. Perhaps Kaowru's body is Adam-based, and only in the shape of Lilim; or perhaps Seele or Adam taught him some advanced AT-field techniques. He seems very good at them; didn't he casually project one of the most powerful AT-fields ever seen?

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Feb 27, 2013 4:17 pm Lavinius wrote: Interesting, but what about Kaworu? He's trapped in a Lilim body, which should be significantly worse than 'tis for Rei, who is the mother of Lilim. And yet Kaworu seems healthy, or at least he doesn't show any angst about it. Perhaps Kaowru's body is Adam-based, and only in the shape of Lilim; or perhaps Seele or Adam taught him some advanced AT-field techniques. He seems very good at them; didn't he casually project one of the most powerful AT-fields ever seen?
